retrieved_docs.content: Dimensional changes from the sintering process of Y-TZP can lead to non-uniform cement space in single copings. The manufacturing process of Y-TZP blanks may influence the sintering shrinkage pattern and, consequently, on the prostheses fit.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Adjacent Stainless Steel Crown With Arch Length Loss/Space Loss
retrieved_docs.content: Reduction of the crown's mesiodistal dimension is frequently required, particularly when mesial drift (often caused by caries) has resulted in arch length loss. Flattening the crown's mesial and distal contact areas using Adam pliers results in a moderate reduction in the mesiodistal dimension. A contralateral upper tooth crown with a smaller size can be used if there is mesial drift in the lower arch. Another form of modification is vertical slicing and spot-welding, an additional segment of a stainless steel band. This is done to increase the perimeter or extend the length. However, the efficacy of the latter modifications remains largely untested. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Manipulation
retrieved_docs.content: The first increment is added to the liquid and mixed using a folding motion for 15 seconds within a limited area on the mixing pad (see Image . Folding Glass Ionomer Cement). The second increment is added to adjust the consistency. The resultant mixture should have a glossy surface (see Image . Freshly Mixed Glass Ionomer Cement). Such a surface indicates that an adequate number of carboxylic ions are available, crucial for forming chemical bonds with the tooth surface. Mixtures with an opaque surface should be discarded (see Image . Poor Mix Adhesion).

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: GIC and its modifications have a wide array of uses. Owing to resiliency, very low shrinkage, and thermal compatibility with tooth structure, these materials have proven to be liners underneath restorative materials like dental composites (sandwich technique). [28] Attributing to the property of bonding to dentin surfaces without removing the smear layer, their biological compatibility, and fluoride release, GICs and modified GICs are the materials of choice in restoring carious teeth in high-risk patients. [29] Because of the ease of placement and snap set property, light-cured GICs are the material of choice for restoration in children. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Technique or Treatment -- Light Cured Sealant
retrieved_docs.content: The tip of the curing light should be positioned 3 to 5 mm from the sealant surface and applied for 10 to 20 seconds to ensure proper polymerization. Maintaining the correct distance and curing time enhances the strength and longevity of the material.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: One of the technical complications reported in the YTZ-P reconstructions is chipping or delamination of the veneering porcelain. The core-veneer interface is a critical factor in the success of layered restorations. The contributing factors for the fracture may include weakness of the veneer material or the core/veneer bond, stresses and distortion due to the veneering process, unsupported veneering porcelain and configuration of the core and veneer, residual stresses arising from a coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch, and rapid cooling rates after heat treatment. The use of high-speed polishing rotary instruments is contraindicated for clinical use because of nickel contamination, phase changes in zirconia, and the resultant surface roughness.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The longevity of glass ionomer cement restorations is affected by occlusal forces, porosities in set cement, desiccation or water absorption during initial setting, and use of the mixed cement after the loss of gloss. Furthermore, larger GIC particles decrease the wear rate; the cement's solubility depends on the amount of cement at the margins.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Gutta Percha
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Ease of manipulation Radiopaque Low toxicity and plasticity Relatively inexpensive [1] [5] [25] Disadvantages: No dentin-bonding properties Material shrinkage upon setting [1] [5] [25] Bioceramic Core Obturation Materials Advantages: Biocompatible Ability to promote biomineralization High fracture resistance Antimicrobial Dimensional stability upon setting [26] [27] Disadvantages: Technically difficult to manipulate Expensive Shorter shelf-life (cv other core materials) [26] [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement (GIC) is also known as man-made dentin because it simulates dentin in terms of modulus of elasticity, resilience, coefficient of thermal expansion, and thermal conductivity. GIC chemically adheres to the tooth structure, is biologically compatible, and has fluoride-releasing properties. [4] [5] These features proclaim GIC an excellent substitute for dentin. [7] However, reduced strength, abrasion resistance, and esthetics contribute to its decreased usage in various clinical scenarios. [4] [5] The material selection for a clinical condition should be made after weighing the merits and demerits of the available materials.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Properties -- Gutta-Percha
retrieved_docs.content: Gutta-percha has many advantages, such as ease of manipulation, radiopacity, low toxicity, and plasticity. However, it does not bond to dentin and shrinks when cooled. [5] [25] Gutta-percha cones are available in standardized 0.02 taper as specified by the International Organization of Standardization and non-standardized sizes. [5] This allows clinicians to mechanically prepare the canal space using various commercial rotary systems using standardized file sizes. When the canal is prepared to a given file size, a gutta-percha cone of the same size can be inserted and adapted to the canal. [1] [5] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The restorations are processed either by soft machining of pre-sintered blanks or by hard machining of fully sintered blocks. The mechanical properties of 3Y-TZP are strongly affected by its grain size. The mechanical properties of zirconia have been reported to be higher than other ceramics for dental applications. Fracture resistance of 6–10 MPa/m1/2, a flexural strength of 900–1200 MPa, and a compression resistance of 2000 MPa have been reported. Zirconia restorations bear an average load of 755 N. Zirconia yields higher fracture loads than alumina or lithium disilicate. Zirconia ceramics yield superior wear behavior and lower antagonistic wear compared to porcelain.

retrieved_docs.content: Once the sealant has set, wipe the surface with a wet cotton pellet to remove the air-inhibited layer of non-polymerized resin. Omitting this step may leave residual material that produces an unpleasant taste in the patient’s mouth, reducing comfort and satisfaction with the procedure.

retrieved_docs.content: Stage of contact lens blank-  It includes a section of clear PMMA, ethyl acrylate, or copolymer of methyl and ethyl acrylate rod or sheet, which has a diameter of 12.7 and 15.9 mm with a thickness of 3 and 6.5 mm Stage of semifinished blank- Contact lens blank having a posterior curve Stage of semifinished blank- This includes an anterior and posterior curve with a polished surface having optical quality Stage of finished corneal contact lens- This is a finished lens with modified peripheral and intermediate areas on the posterior surface and the diameter and edge of the lens. retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Y-TZP has a high fracture toughness and flexural strength. When a crack initiates on the surface of Y-TZP, the stress concentration at the top of the crack causes the tetragonal crystal to transform into a monoclinic crystal, with associated volumetric expansion. In the vicinity of a propagating crack, the stress-induced transformation leads to compressive stress that shields the crack tip from the applied stress and enhances the fracture toughness.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم, a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain sufficient closure during pregnancy, leading to an increased risk of preterm birth or pregnancy loss. The cerclage technique involves placing a suturing material around the cervix to reinforce its structural integrity, thereby preventing premature cervical dilation. This intervention is employed as a therapeutic measure to manage cervical insufficiency and improve obstetric outcomes for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2432
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester, is mapped to short cervical length in pregnancy, indicating that the condition of cervical shortening at any stage of gestation is understood as a short cervical length—a key obstetric measurement used to evaluate the risk of preterm birth and guide patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2025
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical cerclage suture present, polyp of cervix affecting pregnancy, previous operation to cervix affecting pregnancy, and rigid cervix uteri affecting pregnancy are all findings that have the cervix uteri as their anatomic site. These entities represent structural or pathological conditions of the cervix that can influence pregnancy outcomes. A cervical cerclage suture is placed to reinforce a weakened cervix and prevent premature dilation, while a cervical polyp can interfere with implantation or cause bleeding during gestation. Prior cervical surgery may alter cervical integrity and increase the risk of cervical insufficiency, and a rigid cervix can impede normal cervical remodeling required for labor. Recognizing that each of these findings is located in the cervix uteri helps clinicians assess cervical health, anticipate potential obstetric complications, and tailor management strategies to support a safe pregnancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2028
retrieved_docs.content: Malposition, disproportion or other labor complications are recognized as a form of failure to induce labor (فشل تحريض المخاض) and are also classified as causes of a prolonged first stage of labor (مرحلة أولى مطولة من المخاض) as well as a prolonged second stage of labor (مرحلة ثانية مطولة من المخاض). These classifications reflect that abnormal fetal positioning, size mismatch, or other intrapartum problems can impede the initiation of effective uterine contractions and can extend both early and later phases of the birthing process, highlighting the need for careful monitoring and appropriate obstetric interventions to prevent labor dystocia and improve maternal‑fetal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2680
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation are recognized as a related group of adverse perinatal outcomes and are classified together as a distinct clinical category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that children born prematurely with low birth weight have an increased risk of developing reduced height later in life.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2028
retrieved_docs.content: Malposition, disproportion or other labor complications are classified as رَحم ناقص التوتر (uterine inertia), فشل تحريض المخاض (failure of labor induction), مرحلة أولى مطولة من المخاض (prolonged first stage of labor), and مرحلة ثانية مطولة من المخاض (prolonged second stage of labor). These classifications indicate that abnormal fetal positioning, size mismatch, or other labor difficulties can lead to inadequate uterine contractility, unsuccessful attempts to induce labor, and extended durations of both the first and second stages of labor, all of which are critical considerations for obstetric assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2381
retrieved_docs.content: MATERNAL AND FETAL CONDITIONS AFFECTING LABOR AND DELIVERY is a broad classification that includes several specific obstetric entities. Among these are Breech malpresentation successfully converted to cephalic presentation, which represents a fetal position abnormality that has been corrected to the optimal head‑first orientation for delivery. Cervical cerclage suture present denotes the placement of a supportive stitch around the cervix to prevent premature dilation in at‑risk pregnancies. Rigid cervix uteri affecting pregnancy describes a condition in which cervical stiffness impedes normal cervical remodeling and can compromise gestational progress. Tumor of cervix affecting pregnancy refers to cervical neoplasms that may interfere with pregnancy maintenance and the mechanics of labor. Finally, سوء تموضع الجنين (fetal malposition) is another form of abnormal fetal orientation that can impact the course of labor. All of these entities are considered child conditions within the overarching category of MATERNAL AND FETAL CONDITIONS AFFECTING LABOR AND DELIVERY, highlighting their collective relevance to the assessment, management, and outcomes of labor and delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 132
retrieved_docs.content: cesarean section, underscoring the clinical relevance of prior cesarean delivery to pelvic tissue pathology. Unspecified disproportion (unspecified) is hierarchically broader than unspecified indication for care or intervention related to labor and delivery (unspecified), which itself is broader than unspecified malposition or malpresentation (unspecified), illustrating how fetal size discrepancies can progress to specific labor‑delivery concerns such as malposition. Pregnancy observable interprets إجهاض معتاد (recurrent miscarriage) through the lens of uterine scar from previous surgery affecting pregnancy; the uterine scar is mapped to previous cesarean section complicating pregnancy or childbirth, highlighting how scar tissue from a prior cesarean influences current pregnancy assessment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2324
retrieved_docs.content: Induction by cervical dilation and other procedures to assist delivery are closely related interventions used to facilitate childbirth, and they may be considered synonymous in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 165
retrieved_docs.content: bleeding risk, infection, growth restriction, and traumatic delivery in newborn care. Laboratory evaluation, particularly measurement of total hemoglobin concentration, is interpreted against these interrelated conditions to guide diagnosis, risk stratification, and therapeutic decision‑making.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2902
retrieved_docs.content: Uterine function includes the conditions رحم ناقص التوتر and عطالة رحمية ثانوية, which represent forms of reduced uterine tone and secondary uterine inertia, respectively, highlighting specific aspects of impaired uterine contractility that can affect labor and postpartum outcomes.
